vulnerabilities.name: SQL Injection
vulnerabilities.description: SQL injection may be possible. The page results were successfully manipulated using the parameter value '1 AND 1=1'.
vulnerabilities.evidence: You have an error in your SQL syntax
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not trust client side input, even if there is client side validation in place. Use parameterized queries or prepared statements instead of building queries from user-supplied input.

vulnerabilities.name: Cross Site Scripting (Reflected)
vulnerabilities.description: Cross-site Scripting (XSS) - Reflected attack was detected. The attack was carried out via the HTTP GET parameter 'searchFor'.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Phase: Implementation. Use a vetted library or framework that does not allow this weakness to occur or provides constructs that make this weakness easier to avoid. Encode all user-supplied output before returning it to the client.

vulnerabilities.name: Absence of Anti-CSRF Tokens
vulnerabilities.description: No Anti-CSRF tokens were found in a HTML submission form. A cross-site request forgery attack may be possible.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Phase: Architecture and Design. Use a vetted library or framework. For example, use anti-CSRF packages such as the OWASP CSRFGuard.

vulnerabilities.name: Vulnerable JS Library (jQuery 1.4.4)
vulnerabilities.description: The identified library jquery, version 1.4.4 is vulnerable. CVE-2011-4969, CVE-2012-6708, CVE-2015-9251 have been published for this library.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Update jQuery to version 3.5.0 or later.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing Content Security Policy Header
vulnerabilities.description: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

vulnerabilities.name: Cookie Without Secure Flag
vulnerabilities.description: A cookie has been set without the secure flag, which means that the cookie can be accessed via unencrypted connections.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Whenever a cookie contains sensitive information or is a session token, then it should always be passed using an encrypted channel. Ensure that the secure flag is set for cookies containing such sensitive information.

vulnerabilities.name: Cookie Without HttpOnly Flag
vulnerabilities.description: A cookie has been set without the HttpOnly flag, which means that the cookie can be accessed by JavaScript.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Ensure that the HttpOnly flag is set for all cookies.

vulnerabilities.name: Server Leaks Version Information via Server HTTP Response Header
vulnerabilities.description: The web/application server is leaking version information via the Server HTTP response header. Access to such information may facilitate attackers identifying other vulnerabilities your server is subject to.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to suppress the Server header or provide generic details.
